channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Trump-backed Paxton topples Senator Cornyn in Texas primary run-off
item.description: Ken Paxton defeats John Cornyn in Texas race, setting up a November election that could decide control of US Senate.

item.title: Group of women and children with alleged ISIL ties returns to Australia
item.description: Australian Federal Police have not made any arrests but say inquiries are ongoing.

item.title: Brazil’s Flavio Bolsonaro meets with Trump amid troubled presidential bid
item.description: Son of former President Jair Bolsonaro is fighting to recover from a scandal that has rocked his presidential campaign.

item.title: How decision-making happens in Iran
item.description: Iran’s negotiators follow a mandate set by the Supreme National Security Council and approved by the Supreme Leader.

item.title: UN chief ‘deeply concerned’ by Moscow plan for Kyiv strikes
item.description: UN Secretary-General Guterres said he was “deeply concerned” after Russia announced plans to strike Ukraine.

item.title: Iran war live: Israel kills 31 in Lebanon; Tehran blasts US truce violation
item.description: Lebanon's Health Ministry reports that Israeli attacks have killed 3,213 people and injured 9,737 since March 2.

item.title: Is the viral ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ a threat to Modi’s government?
item.description: Al Jazeera’s @avawarrinerr explores the rise of the Cockroach Janta Party.

item.title: Chemical tank rupture kills multiple people in US state of Washington
item.description: Several injured people have been transported to hospitals to be treated for chemical burns.

item.title: Aftermath of deadly Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon
item.description: New videos show widespread destruction in Maarakeh following deadly Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon.

item.title: Four people killed after minibus collides with train in Belgium
item.description: The minibus, carrying nine people, drove through closed crossing barriers during the morning rush hour.

item.title: Climber carries kite with Palestinian children’s dreams to Everest summit
item.description: Climber carries kite with Palestinian children’s dreams to Everest summit

item.title: US picks potential partners for using Cold War-era plutonium as fuel
item.description: Trump gov't plans to make about 20 tonnes of plutonium from dismantled nuclear warheads available to US power firms.

item.title: Trump-led push to redraw Congress maps faces setbacks in Southern states
item.description: State lawmakers reject a new map in South Carolina as judges strike down newly drawn US House districts one in Alabama.

item.title: US Senate confirms veteran naval officer Frank Garcia as top Africa envoy
item.description: The Senate confirmation ends a long vacancy and signals a trade-focused shift in US policy towards Africa.

item.title: Did Trump oversell a broken Iran ceasefire deal?
item.description: The US and Iran appear closer to a broader agreement, but disputes over sanctions relief remain.

item.title: Children perform ‘mini Hajj’ in Gaza as Israel blocks pilgrims
item.description: A Gaza City school taught children how to perform the entire Hajj, as Palestinians miss the pilgrimage for a third year.

item.title: White House proposes NDAs for all US federal workers
item.description: Trump administration threatens legal action against employees who leak, aiming to curb information flow to journalists.

item.title: Russia slams US for not granting visa to diplomat for UN meeting
item.description: Moscow's envoy accuses Washington of failing to honour commitments under the 1947 UN Headquarters Agreement.

item.title: Former Arsenal midfielder Partey named in Ghana squad ahead of World Cup
item.description: Ghana's Thomas Partey, who joined Villareal from Arsenal in 2025, has pleaded not guilty to seven rape charges in UK.

item.title: Armenia signs strategic partnership deal with US as election approaches
item.description: PM Nikol Pashinyan, who deepened ties with US, faces challenge from pro-Russia parties in upcoming parliamentary polls.

item.title: India’s World Cup broadcast hopes boosted by Zee Entertainment talks
item.description: Broadcasting rights for FIFA World Cup 2026 in India have been at a deadlock only weeks ahead of June 11 kickoff.

item.title: Lebanon latest: Netanyahu vows escalation against Hezbollah
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto brings you the latest from southern Lebanon amidst increasing Israeli attacks.

item.title: What has Pope Leo warned about AI – and why that’s significant
item.description: The pontiff presented his encyclical at the Vatican alongside AI experts.

item.title: France’s Kouame becomes youngest male Grand Slam match-winner in 17 years
item.description: At 17, Moise Kouame becomes the youngest winner of a Grand Slam match with French Open win against Marin Cilic.

item.title: Albert Manifold ousted as BP chair over governance and conduct concerns
item.description: BP stock falls by over 4 percent in US and UK markets as governance concerns lead to chairman Albert Manifold's removal.
